title: ferray-io: load reads element-by-element — no bulk read for native endianness
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

NpyElement::read_vec reads each element individually: 'for _ in 0..count { reader.read_exact(&mut buf); }'. For native endianness (no byte swapping needed), the entire data block could be read in a single read_exact call and reinterpreted as a slice of T. NumPy reads the entire data buffer in one fread. For non-native endianness, bulk read + in-place byte swap would still be faster than per-element read+swap. This is the single biggest I/O performance issue.
